title: garlicky quick canadian lamb stew
description: a mouth-watering stew featuring lamb, potatoes, and a hint of garlic. serves 4-6 people.
instructions: heat a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. add the lardons and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
remove the lardons from the pot and set aside. add the lamb pieces and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
remove the lamb from the pot and set aside with the lardons. add the onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
add the garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute. add the potatoes, lamb stock, bay leaves, thyme, salt, and black pepper to the pot.
add the lamb and lardons back to the pot. bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, until the lamb is tender, about 30 minutes.
remove the bay leaves from the pot. serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
